steps.title: Recognize the logical puzzle
steps.content: The passage sets up an apparent paradox: thirteenth-century artifacts are found at a settlement founded in the fourteenth century. If both facts are true, what must logically also be true to make them compatible?

steps.title: Determine the logical requirement
steps.content: For thirteenth-century artifacts to exist at a fourteenth-century-founded settlement, those artifacts cannot have originated at the site—they must have arrived after its founding through trade, migration, or similar means. This is exactly what choice B states. The other options don't resolve the core incompatibility: A discusses how common artifacts from different periods are, C speculates about where the founders came from without addressing the artifact timeline, and D mentions excavation damage to unrelated materials. That matches choice B — B is correct.
